music_description: Download & stream: https://defect.click/NG117D//From a state steeped in house culture, Harry Romero is one of New Jersey’s finest house exports. Emerging in the mid 1990s and remaining one of the genre’s most in demand producers and remixers to this day, he now debuts on the legendary Nu Groove in the first wave of artists to release music for the new era of the label. Delivering exquisite two tracker ‘I Love / The City’, Harry plays tribute to classics from the Nu Groove catalogue with his modern ‘Choo Choo’ twist he’s loved and revered for. House bubbler ‘I Love’ opens the package with depth and warmth to get you in the mood, while ‘The City’ is a little busier in its composition, a spoken-word vocal telling the familiar tale of house music’s ability to unite, while kinetic percussion and glowy synths make this another striking cut.

music_description: Listen every upload first at: https://www.instagram.com/nuggets_1990////Oddysee keep their hot streak alive. This time CRYME lands on the label with 5 club tools designed for maximum dance floor functionality. Titled “7th Element”, the Berlin-based producer finds himself in form with a collection of deep and dubby dance floor grooves, rhythmic programming and sexy vocal-infused house. The record also features a sophisticated slice of steamy, house-laced techno in collaboration with Ben Reymann. Another step in the SEVEN’s co-founder steadily growing repertoire that shows continues upwards progression.  ///Artist: Cryme & Ben Reymann /Track: Stairway Of Desire/EP Title: 7th Element/Label: Oddysee/Catalog #: ODDY003/Genre: House, Tech House/Year: 2026///Pre-order: https://oddysee.bandcamp.com/album/7th-element///https://soundcloud.com/oddyseefm/https://soundcloud.com/ccrryymmee

music_description: It is finally time to reveal a special project! It all started a year ago when Anacalypto shared a file from a relatively unknown producer based in Amsterdam. Immediately hooked by its hypnotic soundscape, my buddy ‪@ejaaje‬ and I went down the rabbit hole in search of more hidden gems, hunting down anything with Angelo’s name on it.///What a crazy goldmine it turned out to be! In the next few uploads, we’ll be unveiling more of the many tracks this guy has produced. We’re kicking things off with the record where it all began and we’re happy and proud to announce that this one will get a well-deserved repress on Anacalypto Records. music_description: Not a blind upload this time, but one of my favorite pieces of wax I own. A record that, in my opinion, still flies way too far under the radar. music_description: Listen every upload first at: https://www.instagram.com/nuggets_1990////When you look up Elevator Music on Discogs, its profile tells you it’s a Swiss hardtrance label. Scrolling through their catalog, you’ll probably come across a cheesy trance compilation on CD, prompting a quick exit to your next dig. But if you take a closer look, you’ll notice there are actually two interesting vinyl releases—both dropped in 1997///Apoll – Mutonk vs. Neurokiller was already unearthed a few years ago, but the slightly more mysterious X-Rhythm EP is still waiting for its moment in the spotlight. This EP definitely feels like an outsider within the label but the other 3 mixes on the record sure give a bit more ravey vibes!///Discogs: https://www.discogs.com/release/2030020-X-Rhythm-Ill-Ride-You///Artist: X-Rhythm/Track: Ill Ride You (Experience Mix)'/EP Title: Ill Ride You /Label: Elevator Music/Catalog #: EM-1201/Genre: House, Progressive House/Year: 1997///Just here to share our passion for good music. music_description: Listen every upload first at: https://www.instagram.com/nuggets_1990////After reaching the 4k mark on YouTube yesterday, it’s time for a little celebration—and what better way than with a special upload! A while back, I already uploaded Clubdeal 1 EP to the channel, but one final gem remained untouched—until now///Without a doubt, the most exceptional release on the Motivator label: Maximizer E.P. music_description: Listen every upload first at: https://www.instagram.com/nuggets_1990////Flower Grooves = Power Grooves///Le Strange, a.k.a. Fred Henderson, was one of the most renowned names in Dutch house music during the '90s. With numerous aliases and projects linked to his name, he left a significant mark on the scene.///In 1996, he launched his own record label, Roxy’s, named after the iconic club where he was a resident DJ at the time. This track is instantly recognizable by the signature vibe that defined Dutch productions of that era—uplifting, energetic, and irresistibly groovy///Artist: Le Strange/Track: Just To Right/EP Title: The Bridge E.P./Label: Flower Grooves/Catalog #: FG009-5/Genre: House/Year: 1997///Just here to share our passion for good music. It felt like the perfect time to listen some of those forgotten records and rediscover why it's in my collection in the first place.///I can't say much about the record itself... There’s no label or artist to research, which only adds to the mystery. Yet, despite the anonymity, I was pleasantly surprised by the vibe it gave off. music_description: CARGO Recording is a project initiated by the IBOAT’s team in Bordeaux. In this first volume, 12 artists deliver their audio definition of the IBOAT Club and submarine dance. ///With previous releases on labels such as Slowciety and Slam City Jams Clint continues his foray into the world of euphoric trance atmospherics ready for any dance floor. music_description: Dear friends and founders of the Amsterdam based platform Oddysee has started a new chapter. The fresh headhonchos invited two newcomers for their first release, the Tel-Aviv based producers Asphalt DJ and Gzardin surface on Oddysee for their anticipated debut. I proudley present to you the title track of the EP before it hit the shelves in November.
